steps.instruction: Find the lowest common denominator (LCD) of 3 and 8.
steps.working: The multiples of 3 are 3, 6, 9, 12, 15, 18, 21, 24, … and the multiples of 8 are 8, 16, 24, … The LCD is 24.

steps.instruction: Convert each fraction to an equivalent fraction with denominator 24.
Add the numerators, keeping the denominator the same.

title: Dividing fractions and simplifying
problem: Calculate $\dfrac{3}{4} \div \dfrac{9}{10}$. Give your answer in simplest form.

steps.instruction: Change the division to multiplication by taking the reciprocal of the second fraction.
Multiply numerators together and denominators together.
Simplify by dividing the numerator and denominator by their highest common factor (HCF = 6).

title: Adding mixed numerals in a word problem
problem: A painter uses $1\dfrac{3}{4}$ litres of paint on Monday and $2\dfrac{1}{3}$ litres on Tuesday. How many litres did the painter use in total?

steps.instruction: Convert each mixed numeral to an improper fraction.
Find the LCD of 4 and 3, then rewrite each fraction with that denominator.
